Accreditation

EAQUALS

EAQUALS (Evaluation and Accreditation of Quality Language Services) is an independent, international professional association that accredits language education institutions by setting and monitoring rigorous quality standards across teaching and learning, curriculum design, assessment, teacher qualifications, academic management, and student services.

Established in 1991 and based in Europe, EAQUALS works in close alignment with the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR) and promotes excellence, transparency, and consistency in language education worldwide.

Through a comprehensive inspection process and ongoing quality assurance mechanisms, EAQUALS accreditation confirms that an institution meets internationally recognised benchmarks for educational quality, ethical practice, and continuous professional development in language teaching and learning.

According to the EAQUALS 2025 Inspection Report

Kadir Has University was inspected by EAQUALS in 2025 and met the high standards required for EAQUALS accreditation. The teaching, the course programmes, as well as the course organisation, the learning resources, testing and evaluation were all judged to be of high quality. It was found that the institution takes great care to protect the welfare of its students and staff, and that all publicity materials produced by the institution are accurate and truthful.
